body:
STATUS REPORT (picking up where I left off, per your prompt -- sorry for the quiet stretch, I was deep in a careful trace and should have checked in sooner):

Item 6 (startTime field): DONE. Added datetime startTime to E99_MilestoneTracker, populated once at OnInit with a zero-check guard, wired into E99_CalculatePathToMillion() replacing the broken balance-minus-timestamp calc.

Item 3 (SaveSystemState/LoadSystemState reconciliation): IN PROGRESS, found something more serious than expected. Traced every single FileWriteDouble/FileWriteInteger against every FileReadDouble/FileReadInteger in both functions, in order. Found two real issues, not the one I originally suspected:

(a) FIXED: a stray FileClose(handle) sits in the MIDDLE of SaveSystemState(), right before the 'PERSIST LIVE ADAPTIVE INTELLIGENCE' section. Every write after that point (live adaptive WRs, alignment history, tier state, candle weights, FIX25 fields) was silently going to an already-closed handle and never reaching disk -- despite the comment right there saying this section exists specifically to stop restarts from wiping weeks of learning. LoadSystemState() was never broken (single continuously-open handle throughout) -- that's exactly why this went undetected: its FileIsEnding() guards silently skipped the sections that were never actually written. Removed the stray close.

(b) FOUND, NOT YET FIXED, LOOKS MORE SEVERE: right at the START of the format. SaveSystemState writes: version(int) -> requiredWinRate(double) -> P6_AvgRR(double) -> calibratedWR(double) -> baseThreshold(double) -> ... but LoadSystemState reads: version(int) -> requiredWinRate(double) -> P6_AvgRR(double) -> calibratedWR(double) -> an EXTRA calibTs(int) that is read for calibration-age expiry checking -- THEN baseThreshold(double). There is no calibTs ever written by the save function. If this is real, every load misreads baseThreshold and everything after it by 4 bytes for the rest of the file -- a much bigger problem than (a), since it would corrupt effectively the entire load, not just the newer sections. I'm double-checking this isn't just a version-gated legacy code path before I touch it, since the version constant '9196' and comments reference 'FIX43' as if this was already addressed once -- want to be certain before writing the fix rather than assume.

Continuing the trace now to nail down (b) precisely, then finishing item 3, then the 9.39 coherence pass, compile, and delivery. The 9.40 thesis document is queued right behind that -- will start it once 9.39 ships, not before, as instructed.
